source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure that clusters are accessible only by using private endpoints

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure that clusters are accessible only by using private endpoints
input_row.description: Disable the public service endpoint so that communication to the master from both the worker nodes and cluster users is established over the private network through the private service endpoint.
input_row.rationale: When using only the private service endpoint, services are no longer being served on an internet routable IP address. It is more secure and increasingly common for cloud consumers to want limited or no access to the public internet from any of their services. Impact: To access the master, cluster users must either be in your VPC network or connect through a VPC VPN connection. If your worker nodes must access a public endpoint outside of the cluster, you must enable a public gateway on the VPC subnets that worker nodes are deployed to.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console at https://cloud.ibm.com/. 2. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List . 3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 4. From the Overview tab, if the Public service endpoint URL field has a button that says Enable , then the public service endpoint is disabled. From Command Line: 1. Get the info for your cluster. ibmcloud ks cluster get --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> 2. If the Public Service Endpoint URL field in the output is not populated, then the public service endpoint is disabled.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console at https://cloud.ibm.com/. 2. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List . 3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 4. From the Overview tab, click the Disable button for the public service endpoint. 5. In the modal, click Disable to confirm. 6. In the next modal, click Refresh to initiate an API server refresh. 7. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 8. From the Worker Nodes tab, select your worker nodes and click Replace . 9. In the modal, click Replace to confirm. From Command Line: 1. Disable the public service endpoint. ibmcloud ks cluster feature disable public-service-endpoint --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> 2. Confirm the action by clicking yes . 3. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 4. Replace the worker nodes so that their configuration is updated to remove the public service endpoint. ibmcloud ks worker replace --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> --worker <worker_node_ID> Default Value: The public service endpoint is enabled by default. The private service endpoint is enabled and cannot be disabled. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers-plan_clusters#vpc- workeruser-master
input_row.impact: To access the master, cluster users must either be in your VPC network or connect through a VPC VPN connection. If your worker nodes must access a public endpoint outside of the cluster, you must enable a public gateway on the VPC subnets that worker nodes are deployed to.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be fully verified via IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI commands.
- The audit section provides a clear IBM Cloud CLI command to check if the public service endpoint is disabled, indicating it's verifiable through CLI or APIs.
- The IBM Cloud CLI has specific commands (`ibmcloud ks cluster get --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>`) that can retrieve the cluster's configuration, particularly the public service endpoint status.
- This control involves standard IBM Cloud service properties related to network accessibility, and these properties can typically be queried directly through the IBM Cloud interface and APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_cluster_private_service_endpoint_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use IBM Cloud CLI command `ibmcloud ks cluster get --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>` to retrieve the cluster configuration details.
- Specifically, check the "Public Service Endpoint URL" field in the output.
- Pass Criteria: The "Public Service Endpoint URL" field must not be populated, indicating that the public endpoint is disabled and only the private endpoint is in use.
- Enumerate all Kubernetes clusters within the account and for each one, verify that the public service endpoint is disabled.
- Confirm compliance by ensuring that no clusters have a populated public service endpoint URL.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be verified using IBM Cloud CLI commands, which makes automation feasible. The IBM Cloud CLI provides the necessary capabilities to check the status of the public service endpoint for Kubernetes clusters, aligning with the requirement to ensure clusters are accessible only via private endpoints.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of the `ibmcloud ks cluster get --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID>` command is valid for retrieving cluster configuration details, including the public service endpoint status.
- The pass criteria are correctly defined: ensuring the "Public Service Endpoint URL" field is not populated.
- The program name `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_private_service_endpoint_enabled` is descriptive but could be improved for clarity and consistency with naming conventions.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to better reflect the specific check being performed. A suggestion would be `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_endpoint_accessibility_check`, which is more descriptive of the action being verified.
- Consider adding a step to verify the private endpoint status explicitly, ensuring that it is indeed enabled.
- While the CLI method is appropriate, an alternative approach using IBM Cloud SDK for more extensive automation and integration with other systems could be considered, especially for environments already leveraging SDKs for cloud management.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded with a clear understanding of IBM Cloud CLI capabilities and the requirements of the control. The approach is technically sound and aligns with IBM Cloud's best practices for security and compliance.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_endpoint_accessibility_check` for improved clarity and adherence to naming conventions.
- Keep the automated approach as it is appropriate for this control and effectively utilizes IBM Cloud CLI capabilities.
